data.task:
Phase 3, Atom A (file 1 of 4): Bring packages/core/tests/runner-deb-triage.test.ts (currently 1091 lines, ~32 tests) into agreement with the unit-test architecture standard (cocoder/standards/test-architecture.md: one behavior area per file; ~600-line soft budget) by SPLITTING it into cohesive themed test files, with ZERO change to test behavior.

WHAT TO DO:
1. Read the file and group its tests by cohesive behavior theme. The natural seams are visible in the describe/test names, e.g.: (a) Deb fault triage (tier-2 disposition, directive-timeout, verify-failed, builder failure/timeout, portable-history commit failure, pane-death, self-committed rejected atom, triage-skipped-when-pane-dead); (b) idle watchdog / continuation nudges; (c) terminal status feed + phase derivation (the nested 'WS1 step 2' describe and the 'writes a live status feed', terminal-snapshot, failed-wrap-projection tests); (d) Deb/Oz nudge delivery + seq tracking. Use your judgment on the final grouping — the goal is each resulting file is one cohesive behavior area and comfortably under the ~600-line budget.
2. Create the new themed files as packages/core/tests/runner-deb-triage-<theme>.test.ts (keep the runner-deb-triage- prefix so they sort together). Move tests verbatim — do not rewrite assertions, rename tests, change timeouts, or alter setup logic.
3. ALL new files must import the SAME existing fixture owner used today (packages/core/tests/runner.test-support.ts and ../src/index.js as the original does). Do NOT create any new *-support.ts file and do NOT copy/duplicate any fixture or helper. If a helper is currently defined inline at the top of runner-deb-triage.test.ts and is now needed by multiple split files, move it into the existing runner.test-support.ts and import it — do not duplicate it.
4. If after splitting the original runner-deb-triage.test.ts is empty, delete it; otherwise leave the residual cohesive tests in it.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA (all required):
- `pnpm --filter @cocoder/core test` is GREEN with the SAME TOTAL TEST COUNT as before your change (run it before and after; report both numbers). No test skipped, removed, or weakened.
- No assertion text or test body changed (pure move). 
- No new *-support.ts file; no duplicated fixture/helper. Each new file imports runner.test-support.ts.
- Each resulting file is under ~600 lines (soft target; if one theme is genuinely cohesive and slightly over, say so explicitly).
- `pnpm --filter @cocoder/core typecheck` (or the repo typecheck) passes.

WHAT MUST NOT BREAK: any other test file; the shared fixture owner's existing exports; import paths elsewhere. Report the exact before/after test counts, the new file list with line counts, and the command output proving green.

data.reason: runner-deb-triage.test.ts (1091L/31 tests) split into 5 themed files all <600L sharing the one runner.test-support.ts; behavior-preserving pure move, suite green 747/747 (100 files), typecheck clean, no new/duplicated fixture.

data.task:
Phase 3, Atom A (file 2 of 4): Split packages/core/tests/runner-founder-stop-resume.test.ts (currently 988 lines, 19 tests) into cohesive themed test files, with ZERO change to test behavior, same as the runner-deb-triage split that just landed (atom 0).

WHAT TO DO:
1. Read the file and group its 19 tests by cohesive behavior theme. Natural seams visible in the test names: (a) ask-founder-continue PARKING and resuming a parked founder decision (consuming the founder answer as continuation); (b) RESUME mechanics — reattaching live stored Oscar/Bob/Deb panes without duplicates, fallback fresh-spawn when panes are dead, launch-prompt selection on resume, late-directive rejection on failed runs, the ticket-0079/run_272 survives-past-orchestrationMs case; (c) FOUNDER-STOP ARTIFACT holding at each boundary (directive boundary, after a passing atom, during Bob execution, while awaiting verify) without dispatch/abandon/quarantine/nudge; (d) HELD-RUN RESUME from a persisted pre-dispatch directive / pre-verdict reissue; (e) ABORT + quarantine behaviors (abort while awaiting directive ends stopped; abort while monitoring Bob / awaiting verify abandons+quarantines the active atom). Use your judgment on the final grouping — each resulting file must be one cohesive behavior area and comfortably under the ~600-line soft budget.
2. Create new files as packages/core/tests/runner-founder-stop-resume-<theme>.test.ts (keep the runner-founder-stop-resume- prefix so they sort together). Move tests VERBATIM — do not rewrite assertions, rename tests, change timeouts, or alter setup.
3. ALL new files import the SAME existing owners the original uses: ../src/index.js, ../src/runner/founder-stop.js, and ./runner.test-support.js. Do NOT create any new *-support.ts file and do NOT duplicate any fixture/helper. If a helper is defined inline at the top of the original and is now needed by multiple split files, move it into the existing runner.test-support.ts and import it — never duplicate.
4. If the original file ends up empty, delete it; otherwise leave the residual cohesive tests in it.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA (all required):
- `pnpm --filter @cocoder/core test` GREEN with the SAME TOTAL TEST COUNT (run before and after; report both; today's baseline is 747). No test skipped/removed/weakened.
- No assertion text or test body changed (pure move).
- No new *-support.ts file; no duplicated fixture/helper. Each new file imports runner.test-support.js.
- Each resulting file under ~600 lines (if one cohesive theme is slightly over, say so explicitly).
- `pnpm --filter @cocoder/core typecheck` passes (exit 0).

WHAT MUST NOT BREAK: any other test file; runner.test-support.ts exports; import paths elsewhere. Report exact before/after test counts, the new file list with line counts, and the command output proving green.

data.reason: runner-founder-stop-resume.test.ts (988L/19 tests) split into 5 themed files all <600L sharing the one runner.test-support.ts; behavior-preserving move, suite green 747/747, typecheck exit 0, no new/duplicated fixture. One dead bare import (abort-quarantine line 3) to be removed in the next atom's directive.

data.task:
Phase 3, Atom A (file 3 of 4) + a one-line cleanup. ZERO change to test behavior throughout.

PART 1 (tiny cleanup from the prior atom): In packages/core/tests/runner-founder-stop-resume-abort-quarantine.test.ts, DELETE the dead bare import on line 3: `import '../src/runner/founder-stop.js'` — it binds nothing and is unused. Remove only that line; touch nothing else in that file.

PART 2 (the split): Split packages/core/tests/tickets.test.ts (currently 855 lines, 25 tests) into cohesive themed files under the ~600-line soft budget.

IMPORTANT — this is the TICKET STORE family, NOT the runner family. Do NOT import runner.test-support.js here; tickets tests are a separate behavior area and currently import only from ../src/index.js. Sibling files already follow this convention (tickets-binding.test.ts, tickets-reconcile.test.ts, tickets-surface-consistency.test.ts) — match their style.

WHAT TO DO:
1. The file has 7 clean top-level describe blocks that are natural seams: 'ticket id allocation', 'ticket loading', 'handled ticket detection', 'ticket index surgery', 'ticket create', 'ticket repoint', 'ticket close'. Group these into cohesive themed files — you may keep closely-related describes together (e.g. id-allocation + loading + handled-detection + index-surgery as a 'core/read' file; create + repoint + close as 'mutations'), as long as each resulting file is one cohesive area and comfortably under ~600 lines. Use your judgment.
2. Name new files packages/core/tests/tickets-<theme>.test.ts (keep the tickets- prefix so they sort with the existing siblings). Move tests VERBATIM — no assertion/timeout/name/setup changes.
3. Inline helpers: `writeRepointFixture` (line ~383) is used only by the repoint tests — move it to whichever file carries the repoint describe. `repoRoot` (line 8) — if it is needed by ONLY ONE resulting file, move it there; if it is needed by TWO OR MORE resulting files, create a SINGLE new owner packages/core/tests/tickets.test-support.ts, export the shared helper(s) from it, and import it from each consumer. Do NOT duplicate any helper across files, and do NOT create more than one ticket support file.
4. If tickets.test.ts ends up empty, delete it; otherwise leave the residual cohesive tests in it.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA (all required):
- `pnpm --filter @cocoder/core test` GREEN with the SAME TOTAL TEST COUNT (baseline 747; run before/after and report both). No test skipped/removed/weakened.
- No assertion text or test body changed (pure move) for both PART 1 and PART 2.
- No duplicated helper; at most ONE new tickets.test-support.ts owner, and only if a helper is genuinely shared by 2+ files. Do NOT import runner.test-support.js.
- Each resulting file under ~600 lines (if one cohesive theme is slightly over, say so explicitly).
- `pnpm --filter @cocoder/core typecheck` passes (exit 0).

WHAT MUST NOT BREAK: the existing tickets-binding/reconcile/surface-consistency siblings; any other test; import paths elsewhere. Report exact before/after test counts, the new file list with line counts, whether you created a tickets.test-support.ts (and why), and the command output proving green.

data.reason: Part1: dead bare import removed from abort-quarantine (1-line diff, nothing else). Part2: tickets.test.ts (855L/25 tests) split into 4 cohesive <600L files in the ticket family (no runner-support import, no new helper duplication), 25->25 preserved; suite green 747/747, typecheck exit 0.

data.task:
Phase 3, Atom A (file 4 of 4, the last split): Split packages/core/tests/runner-wrapup-play.test.ts (currently 767 lines, 22 tests) into cohesive themed files under the ~600-line soft budget, with ZERO change to test behavior. Same pattern as the three runner-* splits already landed this run.

WHAT TO DO:
1. Read the file and group its 22 tests by cohesive behavior theme. Natural seams from the test names: (a) wrap-up Play DISPATCH mechanics (one-atom-then-wrap, headless subprocess pickup + gate-commit, output repaired-once before delivery); (b) OUT-OF-LANE adjudication WI-B1 (unadjudicated auto-escalate, ratified wraps normally); (c) ARCHIVE-READINESS disposition recording (archive-confirmation vs awaiting-founder across: first-directive wrap, bound-open-ticket, no-runnable-signal, target-prefixed Run Status normalization, founder-decision, after-builder-dispatch, ticket wrap 'ask'); (d) AWAITING-FOUNDER closeout (validated wrap with founder decision leaves awaiting-founder; post-wrap awaiting-founder returns without a directive-timeout wait); (e) OUTPUT VALIDATION / fallback (validation disabled when no validator; malformed output falls back honestly + dispatched to Deb on retry failure); (f) CLOSEOUT-BRIEF CONTRACT enforcement (label changes enforced from Play contract; rejects ledger-shaped / priority-ledger briefs; accepts open ticket as next item; permits founder-facing numerals; blocks non-launchable Recommended Next Step). Group into cohesive files (you may keep closely-related themes together) — each resulting file must be one cohesive area and comfortably under ~600 lines. Use your judgment.
2. Name new files packages/core/tests/runner-wrapup-play-<theme>.test.ts (keep the runner-wrapup-play- prefix so they sort together). Move tests VERBATIM — no assertion/timeout/name/setup changes.
3. ALL new files import the SAME existing owners the original uses: ../src/index.js and ./runner.test-support.js. Do NOT create any new *-support.ts file and do NOT duplicate any fixture/helper. If a helper is defined inline at the top of the original and is now needed by multiple split files, move it into the existing runner.test-support.ts and import it — never duplicate.
4. If the original file ends up empty, delete it; otherwise leave the residual cohesive tests in it.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA (all required):
- `pnpm --filter @cocoder/core test` GREEN with the SAME TOTAL TEST COUNT (baseline 747; run before/after and report both). No test skipped/removed/weakened.
- No assertion text or test body changed (pure move).
- No new *-support.ts file; no duplicated fixture/helper. Each new file imports runner.test-support.js.
- Each resulting file under ~600 lines (if one cohesive theme is slightly over, say so explicitly).
- `pnpm --filter @cocoder/core typecheck` passes (exit 0).

WHAT MUST NOT BREAK: any other test file; runner.test-support.ts exports; import paths elsewhere. Report exact before/after test counts, the new file list with line counts, and the command output proving green.

data.reason: runner-wrapup-play.test.ts (767L/22 tests) split into 6 themed files all <600L sharing the one runner.test-support.ts; behavior-preserving move, 22->22 preserved, suite green 747/747, typecheck exit 0, no new/duplicated fixture. Atom A complete: all four >600L files now within the standard.

data.task:
Phase 3, Atom B (the ONLY automation in this priority): Add ONE small binary structural-guard test. It asserts that no test file lives under the cocoder/ governance tree. This is the single objective, threshold-free rule from the unit-test architecture standard (cocoder/standards/test-architecture.md / ADR-0047): tests live next to the code, never under cocoder/. It is explicitly NOT a size enforcer and NOT a duplicate-fixture detector — do not add any line-count, count, or similarity check.

WHAT TO DO:
1. Create ONE new file: packages/core/tests/test-architecture-guard.test.ts. No new runner wiring, no new support file, no production-code changes — just this vitest test.
2. Behavior: derive the install/repo root deterministically from the test file location (use fileURLToPath(import.meta.url) + path join, the same way packages/core/tests/tickets-core.test.ts derives repoRoot — do NOT depend on process.cwd()). The root is three levels up from packages/core/tests (i.e. the directory that contains cocoder/). Recursively walk <root>/cocoder and collect every file whose name matches a test pattern: name ending in .test.ts, .test.tsx, .test.js, .test.jsx, .test.mjs, or .test.cjs (match `.test.` followed by a js/ts extension — equivalently *.test.* with a js/ts-family extension). Assert the collected set is EMPTY.
3. EXCLUSION (mandatory, the one carve-out): skip the cocoder/zArchive/ subtree entirely. It holds frozen point-in-time SOURCE snapshots that legitimately include their original test files (e.g. cocoder/zArchive/source/packages/core/tests/*.test.mjs); those are archived history, not live tests, and must not trip the guard. Also skip any node_modules directory encountered during the walk. Do NOT add any other exclusions — keep the carve-out minimal and self-explanatory with a short inline comment stating WHY zArchive is excluded (frozen source snapshots).
4. Failure message: if the set is non-empty, the assertion message must list the offending repo-relative paths so a future violation is immediately actionable. Use expect(found, message).toEqual([]) or equivalent so the offending paths print.
5. Use node:fs (readdirSync with withFileTypes, recursive walk) — synchronous is fine for a guard. Keep it small (well under ~60 lines).

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA (all required):
- The new guard test PASSES on the current tree (the live cocoder/ tree outside zArchive currently has zero test files, so it must be green now).
- `pnpm --filter @cocoder/core test` GREEN, total test count = 748 (today's 747 + this one new test). Report before/after counts.
- `pnpm --filter @cocoder/core typecheck` passes (exit 0).
- No other file changed; no production code touched; no new support/wiring.

SANITY CHECK to include in your report (do not leave it in the committed test): temporarily confirm the guard actually catches a violation by mentally/locally verifying that if a *.test.ts existed under cocoder/ outside zArchive it would be listed — but the COMMITTED test must only assert the empty set, with the zArchive+node_modules exclusions. Report the new file's full contents, the before/after test counts, and the command output proving green.

data.reason: Single guard test-architecture-guard.test.ts asserts no *.test.* under cocoder/ (excludes zArchive frozen snapshots + node_modules), deterministic root; PROVEN non-tautological (planted probe failed listing its path, passes once removed); threshold-free, no size/dup check; suite green 748/748, typecheck exit 0, only the one new file added.

data.signal: pnpm --dir <install-root> exec cocoder oz archive-priority review-test-architecture-and-componentize

data.waitCondition: wrap-up prepared; final delivery pending landing outcome

data.outcome: Committed — 6 commit(s) on `main`; work is on the active branch by construction. Nothing out of lane.
